title: cassipourea mollis
intro: Cassipourea mollis, also known as the soft cassipourea, is a plant species that belongs to the family Ochnaceae and is native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Cassipourea mollis are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
